item.title: What will you bring back from vacation?
The Smithsonian serves all Americans
item.description: Smithsonian National Museum of American History, American principles and NFL grass, in readers' eyes.

item.title: Social Media Is Making Girls Unhappy | Freya India
item.description: In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” Kate Andrews sits down with Freya India, author of "Girls: Generation Z and the Commodification of Everything." They explore the challenges of coming of age in a chronically online era, discussing how constant digital access affects mental health and self-image among young women.

item.title: How abolishing the NIH would weaken American science
item.description: American science, disability service provider staffing and more, in readers' eyes.

item.title: Children as tech support
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on parenting and technology.

item.title: The DSA can’t hide its extremism
item.description: Its leaders may deny it, but this group has designs far beyond the social safety net.

item.title: One brave Democrat delivers a blunt autopsy of his party
item.description: Joe Cunningham says Dems are losing voters to Trump’s simple ideas.

item.title: The problem with ‘Tax the rich!’
item.description:
A trillion dollars seems like enough money to solve almost anything. We could end world hunger, wipe out medical debt, shrink the federal deficit and still have plenty left over. So when people talk about Elon Musk’s potential trillion-dollar fortune, or the wealth of billionaires more broadly, it’s natural to ask: Why don't we simply tax the rich and use the money to fix our country’s biggest problems?
Host Megan McArdle argues that while taxing billionaires may sound morally obvious, billionaire wealth is often a stock of assets rather than a flow of spendable income — and that distinction makes wealth taxes far more complicated than they appear.

item.title: Putting the Civil Rights Act back on track
item.description: The Trump administration’s fight against unlawful disparate-impact liability is good for business and the Constitution.

item.title: Hanging on
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on workers and automation.

item.title: Is the president too powerful? A constitutional scholar weighs in.
item.description: How did Congress become weaker and what does that mean for U.S. government today? In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” host Damir Marusic talks with American Enterprise Institute scholar Yuval Levin about Congress’s decline, recent Supreme Court decisions and how technology and polarization are reshaping American politics.
